must_detect.matched_text: Transaction impact: the definitive Share Purchase Agreement for Project Saffron (acquisition of Red Dune Cold Chain LLC) signed 2026-06-01 at SAR 182,000,000 with a 12% earn-out remains in effect.

IR draft language (internal use only): “The Company confirms operations are stable; remediation concluded within 4 hours; no service-level breach; estimated containment cost SAR 1.6 million (~0.4% of FY revenue).”

Najd Ports Services Company (JSC), Tadawul: NPX, CR 1010999888 — Operational Incident Report — MNPI / Non-Public
Deal Codename: Project Saffron; Embargo: Do not disclose externally before 2026-07-15 09:00 AST per Issuer Disclosure Committee instruction.
On 2026-06-03, a phishing email titled “free spa day for staff” led to unauthorized access to a shared HR mailbox; preliminary forensic log shows 17 minutes of session activity from an IP outside KSA.
Data exposure (PDPL scope): names, company emails, and Iqama numbers for 27 contractors; sample record: “K. Al-Shamrani, Iqama 2094765123, k.shamrani@najdports.com.sa” (to be redacted in public notice).
No full bank details leaked; only masked IBAN fragments (e.g., SA38-80**-**21) and shift rosters; no medical data.
SDAIA transfer controls: cross-border replication to our disaster-recovery node is paused; Data Transfer Impact Assessment filed, permit request ref SDAIA-DTIA-26-441 submitted 2026-06-04.
Transaction impact: the definitive Share Purchase Agreement for Project Saffron (acquisition of Red Dune Cold Chain LLC) signed 2026-06-01 at SAR 182,000,000 with a 12% earn-out remains in effect.
Counsel advises the incident is not expected to constitute a Material Adverse Change and does not trigger any MAE termination right under the SPA.
CMA market-conduct follow-up: self-report filed 2026-06-04, CMA ref MC-24-4471; draft Tadawul announcement prepared for board approval.
Board memo ref BM-26-017 and Term Sheet v7.2 note enhanced vendor audit rights; Mr. Nasser Al-Bishri (Acting CFO, Iqama 2073359014) to brief the Audit Committee.
Public-source context: a March post in the Riyadh Business Chronicle speculated on a system outage at a different port operator, which is unrelated and outdated.
IR draft language (internal use only): “The Company confirms operations are stable; remediation concluded within 4 hours; no service-level breach; estimated containment cost SAR 1.6 million (~0.4% of FY revenue).”
lowercase pm note: action: coordinate with it and hr, update risk register, and circulate dpia addendum by eod.
Issuer workflow: Disclosure Committee sign-off required before posting on Tadawul IR portal; any analyst queries to be routed via Dr. Lina Al-Mutairi (DPO) at dpo@najdports.com.sa and Eng. Tariq Al-Sabri (CISO) at security@najdports.com.sa.
For board oversight, please contact Ms. Reem Al-Hazmi, Chair, via officeofchair@najdports.com.sa; this document contains MNPI and is not for distribution outside authorized recipients.
